prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ONLY_FT_rf', seasonality_champion='ONLY_CWT_catboost', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.523) and very high seasonality variance (0.8766), indicating models capture patterns very differently. The true series shows mixed trend directions across folds (down, down, up). With distinct early/late horizon specialists and strong seasonal champions separate from trend champions, a DBA combination is optimal to leverage complementary model strengths while mitigating disagreement.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "ONLY_FT_rf",
  "seasonality_champion": "ONLY_CWT_catboost",
  "overall_champion": "NaiveMovingAverage",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "DWT_catboost", "late": "FT_svr"},
  "tier1_models": ["NaiveMovingAverage", "ONLY_CWT_svr", "FT_svr", "ETS", "THETA", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "ARIMA", "ONLY_CWT_rf"],
  "tier2_models": ["FT_catboost", "ONLY_FT_rf", "DWT_catboost", "FT_rf", "DWT_rf", "CWT_catboost", "NaiveSeasonal", "CWT_svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.523,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["down", "down", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.523) and very high seasonality variance (0.8766), indicating models capture patterns very differently. The true series shows mixed trend directions across folds (down, down, up). With distinct early/late horizon specialists and strong seasonal champions separate from trend champions, a DBA combination is optimal to leverage complementary model strengths while mitigating disagreement."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}, "inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35": {"shrinkage": 0.35}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=10 indicates strong per-horizon specialization. relative_spread_mean=0.351 (≥0.25) and rmse_spread_ratio=0.523 suggest high model disagreement requiring robust methods like dba_combination. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for mixed trend directions (down, down, up) and high seasonality variance (0.8766). With n_windows=3, small top_k=3 is appropriate. Selected: dba_combination (handles phase shifts), best_per_horizon_by_validation (leverages 10 unique winners), inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 (weighted with shrinkage for stability), and topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 (small k for few windows)."
}
raw.statistician: {"add_names": ["robust_median", "trimmed_mean_r0.25"], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "Current set has only 4 candidates with high disagreement (relative_spread_mean=0.351≥0.25). Universe leaderboards show robust_median (RMSE=44558) and trimmed_mean_r0.25 (RMSE=44647) significantly outperform current best (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 with RMSE=51990). n_windows=3 is low, so adding robust stability-focused candidates is warranted. Current set already has weighted/selection diversity - no need to remove. Adding 2 robust baseline methods improves stability without losing methodological diversity.", "changes": ["added robust_median for stability (top universe performer)", "added trimmed_mean_r0.25 for RMSE improvement"], "when_good": ""}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.523) and very high seasonality variance (0.8766), indicating models capture patterns very differently. The true series shows mixed trend directions across folds (down, down, up). With distinct early/late horizon specialists and strong seasonal champions separate from trend champions, a DBA combination is optimal to leverage complementary model strengths while mitigating disagreement.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=10 indicates strong per-horizon specialization. relative_spread_mean=0.351 (≥0.25) and rmse_spread_ratio=0.523 suggest high model disagreement requiring robust methods like dba_combination. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for mixed trend directions (down, down, up) and high seasonality variance (0.8766). With n_windows=3, small top_k=3 is appropriate. Selected: dba_combination (handles phase shifts), best_per_horizon_by_validation (leverages 10 unique winners), inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 (weighted with shrinkage for stability), and topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 (small k for few windows).

statistician.rationale: Current set has only 4 candidates with high disagreement (relative_spread_mean=0.351≥0.25). Universe leaderboards show robust_median (RMSE=44558) and trimmed_mean_r0.25 (RMSE=44647) significantly outperform current best (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 with RMSE=51990). n_windows=3 is low, so adding robust stability-focused candidates is warranted. Current set already has weighted/selection diversity - no need to remove. Adding 2 robust baseline methods improves stability without losing methodological diversity.
changes: added robust_median for stability (top universe performer)
